steps.title: Find the first slope
steps.content: Rewrite the equation for line $s$ in slope-intercept form: $$6x+2y=0$$ $$2y=-6x$$ $$y=-3x$$ Line $s$ has slope $-3$.

steps.title: Use the perpendicular slope
steps.content: A line perpendicular to a line with slope $-3$ has slope $\frac{1}{3}$. Since line $t$ passes through $(5,0)$, its equation is $$y=\frac{1}{3}(x-5)$$

steps.title: Substitute the second point
steps.content: The point $(k,\frac{4}{3})$ lies on line $t$, so substitute $y=\frac{4}{3}$: $$\frac{4}{3}=\frac{1}{3}(k-5)$$ $$4=k-5$$ $$k=9$$ The answer is 9.
